items.briefing:
A ransomware group known as "The Gentlemen" has had its own data leaked publicly, a reminder that cybercriminal groups themselves face internal threats — though their victims remain at risk in the meantime. Separately, Chinese-made deepfake software is being used to power convincing scams, making it increasingly difficult to verify someone's identity over video calls or in digital communications.

For Australian staff, be cautious of unexpected video or voice contact requesting sensitive information or urgent action — deepfake technology means appearances alone can no longer be fully trusted. When in doubt, verify requests through a separate, known contact method before responding.
